MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. GORDON WALTERS, Esq. of Berding & Weil LLP (tel: 925-838-2090) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. MATTHEW LADNER, Esq. of Troutman Pepper Locke LLP (tel: 213-928-9800) for Defendant Uponor, Inc.
Atty. DANIEL CRIBBS, Esq. of Chapman Glucksman, P.C. (tel: 310-207-7722) for Defendant Swinerton Builders.
Atty. ROBERTA NALBANDIAN, Esq. of Tyson Mendes (tel: 628-253-5070) for Defendant Broadway Mechanical.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT UPONOR, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Uponor. Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied.

Uponor has failed to satisfy its burden that either of the plaintiffs agreed to arbitrate any of their claims against Uponor.

As a separate and independent ground for denying the motion, even if the parties had an agreement to arbitrate, the court exercises its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure 1281.2(c) to deny arbitration. Section 1281.2(c) provides that the court may deny arbitration where "[a] party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action or special proceeding with a third party, arising out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact." There is no showing that the general contractor, subcontractors, and other supplier defendants are subject to an agreement to arbitrate. Significantly, there is no showing that any of the defendants are subject to Uponor's agreement to arbitrate, which requires arbitration in Minnesota.There is a significant possibility of conflicting rulings because one adjudicator could find that the contractors caused the construction defects while another could find that defective products caused the harm. Moreover, discovery will likely be more expeditious and efficient if all of the claims remain in one forum.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
